summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #205 (Group agnostic core Rust component), establish a Codex review-wait skill, and add a lighter `cargo xci --profile fast` pre-push gate to reduce the local CI cycle from ~25 min to ~5 min.
summary.outcome: All three objectives completed: Group component shipped in PR #648 with 33 unit tests + 5 snapshots at 100% coverage, `waiting-for-codex-review` skill landed in `.agents/skills/`, and `cargo xci --profile fast` (10-step subset, ~1m24s wall-clock) implemented with alias `cargo xci-fast`. Two Codex review rounds were addressed and resolved before final push.

repo: CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md — edits must target the real file at AGENTS.md, not the symlink path.
`.claude/skills/` is a symlink resolving into `.agents/skills/` — repo-scoped skills must be written to `.agents/skills/`.
`separator.rs` is the canonical template for stateless utility components (Props + Api + ConnectApi, no state machine, no Service).
Secondary context structs use descriptive non-prefixed names (`GroupContext`, not `Context`) per foundation 08 §8.4 so consumers write `group::GroupContext`.

`aria-readonly` is NOT a WAI-ARIA 1.2 global state — it is role-specific (valid on textbox, checkbox, etc.) and must NOT be emitted on `role=group|region|presentation`. Only `aria-disabled` and `aria-invalid` are WAI-ARIA §6.5 globals.
Post-implementation-audit skill is mandatory per CLAUDE.md §7 and runs three phases: spec/impl drift, iterative 'anything else?' rounds (≥2 clean), and test-coverage audit.
Snapshot fixtures require `cargo insta accept` after the first test run and the PR must receive the `snapshot-reviewed` label.
`pub mod group;` in `utility/mod.rs` must be positioned alphabetically (between `form_submit` and `heading`).
Pre-existing `cargo doc` warnings exist in the workspace and are not gated by `cargo xci` — only new doc warnings in changed files matter.
Codex bot login is `chatgpt-codex-connector` (case-insensitive substring `codex` for discovery).
Codex does not reliably auto-trigger on new pushes — always post `@codex review` explicitly after each push.
Codex signals 'reviewing' by adding 👀; signals 'approved' by replacing 👀 with 👍; signals 'changes requested' by dropping 👀 and posting threads (no 👍 added).
`cargo xci` full profile runs 20 steps (~25 min locally); the new fast profile runs 10 steps in ~1m24s — skips Coverage, FeatureMatrix×5, I18nBrowser, DomBrowser, Release, AdapterParity.

code.finding: Canonical template for stateless utility components — identical structure to Group minus the Context struct.
Reference implementation for a secondary Context struct (FieldContext pattern).
`Direction::as_html_attr()` — used to emit the `dir` attribute from the `dir: Option<Direction>` prop.
Foundation 08 §8.4 naming rule: secondary contexts use descriptive non-prefixed names (`GroupContext`, `QueueContext`).
`Step` enum and `PIPELINE_ORDER` constant live here. Added `Profile { Full, Fast }` enum and `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` constant alongside them.
`Command::Ci` variant definition — added `--profile <full|fast>` flag here with `default_value_t = Profile::Full`.
Codex first-pass review flagged this line — original impl emitted `aria-readonly` here, which is invalid ARIA on role=group/region/presentation (not a WAI-ARIA global state).

workflow: TDD order is enforced by workspace convention: write the failing test first, confirm red, then land the fix.
Monitor tool provides event-driven polling for Codex reaction changes without busy-waiting — emit `[needs_action]` on 👀 drop + threads, `[approval]` on 👍.
Spec wins over issue when they contradict — issue test bullets that contradict the spec are dropped, not implemented.
When impl and spec diverge and the impl is the better outcome, update the spec to match (spec is documentation, impl is ground truth after deliberate decision).

Codex review workflow: push → `@codex review` comment → poll reactions → address threads (TDD-first, no coverage regression) → push fix → resolve thread via GraphQL → post `@codex review` again → loop.
`cargo xci --profile fast` is now the routine pre-push gate; full `cargo xci` reserved for final pre-PR run or large refactors.
Mutation testing (`cargo mutants`) is part of the Phase 3 audit — 0 missed mutations is the bar alongside 100% line/region coverage.
Issue is moved to 'In Progress' on the project board at implementation start; stays there until merged (not closed at PR open).

friction: Spec §3.1 and §1.2 code example contradicted each other on whether state attrs emit for role=presentation — required an explicit planning decision before implementation could start.
Issue #205's test bullets listed `label`/`label_id` props that don't exist in the spec, requiring reconciliation and dropped bullets.
Pre-existing `cargo doc` warnings in the workspace caused false alarm confusion about whether the doc-check step was a new failure.

`aria-readonly` was incorrectly grouped with WAI-ARIA global states in the initial spec edit — Codex caught this in the first review pass. Required a second round: implementation fix, snapshot regeneration, spec prose correction, doc-comment fix.
A commit landed before the user's review message arrived (async timing) — required a soft-reset to restore staged state for re-inspection.
Codex does not reliably auto-trigger on new pushes, making the `@codex review` explicit comment a required step rather than an optional fallback.
`cargo xci` snapshot orphan check failed when run with a crate filter (`-p ars-components`) because other crates' snapshots appeared unreferenced — required running without the filter.
open_items: Leptos adapter for Group component (`ars-leptos`) — deferred to a separate task on the project board.
Dioxus adapter for Group component (`ars-dioxus`) — deferred to a separate task.
Wiring `GroupContext` consumption into existing child components (Button, TextField, etc.) — deferred pending adapter components.
`--exclude <step>` flag for ad-hoc subtraction from a `cargo xci` profile — deferred as a follow-up to the fast-profile feature.
Pre-push git hook to auto-run `cargo xci-fast` — deferred; current enforcement is via CLAUDE.md habit rather than a hook.
Flipping `cargo xci` no-arg default to `fast` profile — deferred pending telemetry on whether anyone uses the full local run.
Pre-existing `cargo doc` warnings in the workspace are unaddressed tech debt not introduced by this PR.
Final Codex doc-fix commit (`Props::read_only` doc correction) was committed locally but transcript ends before it was pushed and the thread resolved — that push and resolution is still pending.
